explanation_en: 'Kriterium' means 'criterion' (a standard for judging). Neuter, takes 'das'. Plural: 'Kriterien'.

question.de: _____ Kriterium ist entscheidend für die Auswahl.
question.en: The criterion is decisive for the selection.

explanation_en: 'Kriterium' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The most important criterion is quality.

explanation_en: 'Kriterium' means 'criterion'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: The applicants must meet several criteria.

explanation_en: 'erfüllen' takes accusative. Plural of 'Kriterium': 'Kriterien' (Latin-origin noun, irregular plural).
'Merkmal' means 'characteristic' or 'feature'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.de: _____ Merkmal dieser Tierart ist der rote Schwanz.
question.en: The characteristic of this animal species is the red tail.

explanation_en: 'Merkmal' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The feature of good leadership is empathy.

explanation_en: 'Merkmal' means 'feature'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.de: Wir analysieren die typischen _____ dieser Textsorte.
question.en: We are analysing the typical features of this text type.

explanation_en: 'analysieren' takes accusative. Plural of 'Merkmal': 'Merkmale' (adds -e).
'Phänomen' means 'phenomenon'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.de: _____ Phänomen ist noch nicht vollständig erklärt.
question.en: The phenomenon has not yet been fully explained.

explanation_en: 'Phänomen' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The phenomenon of the rainbow fascinates me.

explanation_en: 'Phänomen' means 'phenomenon'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: The researchers are investigating this strange phenomenon.

explanation_en: 'untersuchen' takes accusative. Neuter: 'dieses Phänomen' (neuter accusative demonstrative; noun unchanged).
'Prinzip' means 'principle'. Neuter, takes 'das'. Plural: 'Prinzipien'.

question.en: The principle of equality applies universally.

explanation_en: 'Prinzip' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ der Nachhaltigkeit leitet unser Handeln.
question.en: The principle of sustainability guides our actions.

explanation_en: 'Prinzip' means 'principle'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: He holds fast to his principles.

explanation_en: 'festhalten an' takes dative. Plural dative of 'Prinzip': 'Prinzipien' (Latin irregular plural).
'Risiko' means 'risk'. Neuter, takes 'das'. Plural: 'Risiken'.

question.en: The risk is calculable.

explanation_en: 'Risiko' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The risk of failure is high.

explanation_en: 'Risiko' means 'risk'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: We must carefully weigh up the risk.

explanation_en: 'abwägen' takes accusative. Neuter: 'das Risiko' (unchanged in accusative).
'Scheitern' is a nominalised infinitive meaning 'failure' or 'failing'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.en: Failure is not the end.

explanation_en: 'Scheitern' is a nominalised infinitive — neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The failure of the project was foreseeable.

explanation_en: 'Scheitern' means 'failure' (the act of failing). It is the neuter subject here.

question.de: Aus dem _____ kann man viel lernen.
question.en: You can learn a lot from failure.

explanation_en: 'aus dem Scheitern' — 'aus' takes dative. Neuter dative: 'dem Scheitern'. Noun unchanged.
'Ansatz' means 'approach' or 'starting point'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The approach is very innovative.

explanation_en: 'Ansatz' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The approach to the solution is promising.

explanation_en: 'Ansatz' means 'approach'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.en: We are pursuing a new approach.

explanation_en: 'verfolgen' takes accusative. 'einen neuen Ansatz' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'.
'Aspekt' means 'aspect'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The aspect was not taken into consideration.

explanation_en: 'Aspekt' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The legal aspect is particularly important.

explanation_en: 'Aspekt' means 'aspect'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.en: We must carefully analyse every aspect.

explanation_en: 'analysieren' takes accusative. 'jeden Aspekt' — masculine accusative: 'jeden' before unchanged noun.
'Aufwand' means 'effort' or 'expenditure'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The effort is very high.

explanation_en: 'Aufwand' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ für das Projekt ist enorm.
question.en: The effort for the project is enormous.

explanation_en: 'Aufwand' means 'effort'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.de: Es lohnt sich nicht, diesen _____ zu betreiben.
question.en: It is not worth making this effort.

explanation_en: 'betreiben' takes accusative. 'diesen Aufwand' — masculine accusative uses 'diesen'. Noun unchanged.
'Begriff' means 'term' or 'concept'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The term is hard to define.

explanation_en: 'Begriff' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The term 'sustainability' is ubiquitous today.

explanation_en: 'Begriff' means 'term'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.en: I don't understand this term.

explanation_en: 'verstehen' takes accusative. 'diesen Begriff' — masculine accusative uses 'diesen'. Noun unchanged.
'Einwand' means 'objection'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The objection was not heeded.

explanation_en: 'Einwand' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Sein _____ gegen den Plan ist berechtigt.
question.en: His objection to the plan is justified.

explanation_en: 'Einwand' means 'objection'. After 'sein' (masculine nominative), the noun is unchanged.

question.de: Er erhob einen _____ gegen den Beschluss.
question.en: He raised an objection against the resolution.

explanation_en: 'erheben' takes accusative. 'einen Einwand' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'. Noun unchanged.
'Kontext' means 'context'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.de: _____ Kontext ist wichtig für das Verstehen.
question.en: The context is important for understanding.

explanation_en: 'Kontext' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The historical context must be taken into consideration.

explanation_en: 'Kontext' means 'context'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.de: Das Wort hat in diesem _____ eine andere Bedeutung.
question.en: The word has a different meaning in this context.

explanation_en: 'in diesem Kontext' — 'in' with dative. Masculine dative: 'diesem Kontext'.
'Rückgang' means 'decline' or 'decrease'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The decline in sales figures worries us.

explanation_en: 'Rückgang' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ der Bevölkerungszahl ist ein Problem.
question.en: The decline in the population figure is a problem.

explanation_en: 'Rückgang' means 'decline'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.en: The economy is recording a sharp decline.

explanation_en: 'verzeichnen' takes accusative. 'einen starken Rückgang' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'.
'Schwerpunkt' means 'focus' or 'main priority'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.de: _____ Schwerpunkt der Diskussion ist die Digitalisierung.
question.en: The focus of the discussion is digitalisation.

explanation_en: 'Schwerpunkt' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The focus is on quality assurance.

explanation_en: 'Schwerpunkt' means 'focus'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.de: Wir setzen einen neuen _____ in der Forschung.
question.en: We are setting a new focus in research.

explanation_en: 'setzen' takes accusative. 'einen neuen Schwerpunkt' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'.
'Standpunkt' means 'standpoint' or 'point of view'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The standpoint is understandable.

explanation_en: 'Standpunkt' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Von meinem _____ aus ist das falsch.
question.en: From my point of view, that is wrong.

explanation_en: 'von meinem Standpunkt aus' — 'von' takes dative. Masculine dative: 'meinem Standpunkt'. Noun unchanged.

question.en: She defended her standpoint convincingly.

explanation_en: 'verteidigen' takes accusative. 'ihren Standpunkt' — masculine accusative uses 'ihren'.
'Umfang' means 'scope', 'extent', or 'circumference'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.de: _____ Umfang des Projekts ist unterschätzt worden.
question.en: The scope of the project has been underestimated.

explanation_en: 'Umfang' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ der Studie ist sehr breit.
question.en: The scope of the study is very broad.

explanation_en: 'Umfang' means 'scope'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.de: Wir müssen den _____ der Arbeit reduzieren.
question.en: We need to reduce the scope of the work.

explanation_en: 'reduzieren' takes accusative. 'den Umfang' — masculine accusative uses 'den'. Noun unchanged.
'Vorwurf' means 'accusation' or 'reproach'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The accusation is groundless.

explanation_en: 'Vorwurf' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The accusation of corruption is serious.

explanation_en: 'Vorwurf' means 'accusation'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.en: He firmly rejected the accusation.

explanation_en: 'zurückweisen' takes accusative. 'den Vorwurf' — masculine accusative uses 'den'. Noun unchanged.
'Widerspruch' means 'contradiction'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The contradiction is obvious.

explanation_en: 'Widerspruch' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ in seiner Argumentation fällt sofort auf.
question.en: The contradiction in his argument is immediately apparent.

explanation_en: 'Widerspruch' means 'contradiction'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.de: Sie duldet keinen _____ gegen ihre Entscheidungen.
question.en: She tolerates no contradiction of her decisions.

explanation_en: 'dulden' takes accusative. 'keinen Widerspruch' — masculine accusative uses 'keinen'.
'Überblick' means 'overview'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: She still lacks the overview.

explanation_en: 'Überblick' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ über das Thema fehlt mir noch.
question.en: I still lack an overview of the topic.

explanation_en: 'Überblick' means 'overview'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.en: This report gives a good overview.

explanation_en: 'geben' takes accusative. 'einen guten Überblick' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'.
'Kompromiss' means 'compromise'. Masculine, takes 'der'.

question.en: The compromise was accepted by everyone.

explanation_en: 'Kompromiss' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.en: The compromise was hard to reach.

explanation_en: 'Kompromiss' means 'compromise'. It is the masculine subject here.

question.en: We need to find a compromise.

explanation_en: 'finden' takes accusative. 'einen Kompromiss' — masculine indefinite accusative uses 'einen'.
'Rücksicht' means 'consideration for others'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: Consideration for others is important.

explanation_en: 'Rücksicht' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ auf andere Menschen fehlt ihm völlig.
question.en: He completely lacks consideration for other people.

explanation_en: 'Rücksicht' means 'consideration'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Er nimmt keine _____ auf die Gefühle anderer.
question.en: He shows no consideration for others' feelings.

explanation_en: 'Rücksicht nehmen auf' is a fixed phrase meaning 'to show consideration for'. 'keine Rücksicht' — accusative after 'nehmen'.
'Sorgfalt' means 'care' or 'diligence'. Feminine, takes 'die'. No common plural form.

question.de: _____ Sorgfalt ist bei dieser Arbeit nötig.
question.en: Care is needed for this work.

explanation_en: 'Sorgfalt' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ bei der Ausführung ist entscheidend.
question.en: The care in the execution is decisive.

explanation_en: 'Sorgfalt' means 'care'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Er arbeitet mit großer _____ und Genauigkeit.
question.en: He works with great care and precision.

explanation_en: 'mit großer Sorgfalt' — 'mit' takes dative. Feminine dative: 'großer Sorgfalt' (adj. strong ending without article).
'Beziehung' means 'relationship'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The relationship between the two is complicated.

explanation_en: 'Beziehung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ zwischen den Ländern ist gut.
question.en: The relationship between the countries is good.

explanation_en: 'Beziehung' means 'relationship'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Er pflegt eine gute _____ zu seinen Nachbarn.
question.en: He maintains a good relationship with his neighbours.

explanation_en: 'pflegen' takes accusative. 'eine gute Beziehung' — feminine indefinite accusative: 'eine'.
'Erwartung' means 'expectation'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The expectation was very high.

explanation_en: 'Erwartung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ der Kunden muss erfüllt werden.
question.en: The expectation of customers must be met.

explanation_en: 'Erwartung' means 'expectation'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: The result exceeded all expectations.

explanation_en: 'übertreffen' takes accusative. Plural: 'alle Erwartungen' (adds -en to the singular form).
'Stärke' means 'strength'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The strength of his arguments convinced everyone.

explanation_en: 'Stärke' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ des Teams liegt in seiner Vielfalt.
question.en: The strength of the team lies in its diversity.

explanation_en: 'Stärke' means 'strength'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: We should use our strengths.

explanation_en: 'nutzen' takes accusative. Plural: 'unsere Stärken' (adds -n to the singular form).
'Schwäche' means 'weakness'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.de: _____ Schwäche des Plans ist die Finanzierung.
question.en: The weakness of the plan is the financing.

explanation_en: 'Schwäche' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The weakness of the argument is obvious.

explanation_en: 'Schwäche' means 'weakness'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Er kennt seine _____ und arbeitet daran.
question.en: He knows his weaknesses and works on them.

explanation_en: 'kennen' takes accusative. Plural: 'seine Schwächen' (adds -n to the singular form).
